mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or event-study alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in monthly retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative 60-day return in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing and utility output that directly supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >65% of months where INDPRO increases, or the signal reverses direction during inventory accumulation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels combined with persistently low federal funds rates compress real yields and raise expectations for abrupt monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 260, or out-of-sample predictive accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth provides a fundamental floor for corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as markets price in macroeconomic stability.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters following positive GDPC1 growth, or the signal shows zero predictive power in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payrolls decline exceeding 200,000 positions signals severe labor market contraction that triggers recession pricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in >60% of triggered months, or the threshold fails to separate recessionary from expansionary periods in historical backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate synchronized supply-side capacity and demand-side spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through earnings upgrades.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of observations, or the joint effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a panel regression.
